title: RNN, LSTM, GRU — and why we left them
focus: Vanishing gradients, LSTM gates, why transformers won.

items.label: Number OF Islands
interviewQuestions: Compare DFS, BFS, and Union-Find — pick one and defend it.
What if the grid is huge and streamed (rows arrive one at a time)?

items.label: Clone Graph
interviewQuestions: Is this BFS, DFS, or Union-Find? Defend the choice over the other two.
Walk through complexity in terms of V and E. Where do those costs come from?
How would you handle disconnected components, self-loops, or duplicate edges?

items.label: RNN forward + truncated BPTT
interviewQuestions: Why do vanilla RNNs struggle with long-term dependencies?
Walk through truncated backprop-through-time — why is it the practical default?

items.label: LSTM cell: gates and cell state
interviewQuestions: Walk through an LSTM cell: forget, input, output gates and cell state.
How does the cell state help with vanishing gradients?

items.label: GRU vs LSTM
interviewQuestions: When would you pick GRU over LSTM?
What does GRU's update gate do that LSTM splits across forget + input gates?
